description: Queen Patch 5 (26 February 2020) — Alliance Limit Test (Siphoned Energy Drain), Zerg Debuff Changes, Guild/Alliance Cooldowns, Fast Travel to Home, Hideout Item Retrieval, Resource Changes, Dungeon Loot Changes, Fixes.

sections.heading: Siphoned Energy Drain
sections.description: First part of the Alliance Limit Test. A debuff that gives reduced Siphoned Energy gains to all alliances and unallied single guilds that have ten or more territories, with each additional territory beyond ten further decreasing energy earned by 5%. Based on territories only — does not include Castles or Castle Outposts. The second part (Silver / Fame Penalty) will go into effect with Queen Patch 6.
items: Gives reduced Siphoned Energy gains to all alliances and unallied single guilds with ten or more territories, with each additional territory beyond ten further decreasing energy earned by 5%
Based on territories only — does not include Castles or Castle Outposts
sections.searchable_text: Siphoned Energy Drain Alliance Limit Test alliances unallied guilds territories 5% Castles Castle Outposts Silver Fame Penalty Queen Patch 6

sections.heading: Smart Cluster Queue Changes
sections.searchable_text: Smart Cluster Queue Minimum Item Power 800 700 high IP gear

subsections.heading: Smart Cluster Queue
subsections.searchable_text: Smart Cluster Queue Minimum Item Power 800 700 high IP gear emphasis

changes.raw_text: Minimum Item Power: 800 → 700. This reduces the Smart Cluster Queue's emphasis on high IP gear.
notes: This reduces the Smart Cluster Queue's emphasis on high IP gear

sections.heading: Zerg Debuff Changes
sections.description: The player thresholds for the Zerg Debuff ("Disarray") have been adjusted to more quickly ramp up as army size increases. Also fixed an issue where the tooltip was incorrectly displaying the same value for defense and damage reduction.
items: New thresholds vs old thresholds for Damage and Defense vs Players (selected key values): 26 (was 25): -1% | 30 (was 35): -3% | 40 (was 45): -5% | 50 (was 100+): -6% to -16% | 101 (was 170): -30% | 201 (was 255): -47% | 210–255 (new, no old equivalent): -48% to -53%
sections.searchable_text: Zerg Debuff Disarray player thresholds damage defense reduction tooltip army size new old values

subsections.heading: Disarray Threshold Table
subsections.searchable_text: Disarray Zerg Debuff player threshold damage defense vs players new old values table

notes: Thresholds now ramp up more quickly as army size increases
New entries extend the table to -53% (previously capped at -47%)
Fixed tooltip incorrectly displaying the same value for defense and damage reduction

sections.heading: Guild and Alliance Cooldown Changes
items: Introduced a 3-day cooldown to re-joining the same guild after leaving, to prevent exploiting short-term drops of guild/alliance affiliation to negate Disarray effects during battles
Increased the cooldown for joining a new guild from 2 to 3 days to prevent workarounds of this system
Introduced a 3-day cooldown for leaving and rejoining the same alliance, alongside the existing 7-day cooldown on joining a new alliance
sections.searchable_text: Guild Alliance Cooldown re-joining same guild 3 days joining new guild 2 days 3 days leaving rejoining alliance 7 days Disarray Zerg Debuff exploit

sections.heading: Fast Travel to Home
items: Players can now fast travel to a preset "Home" point when not carrying any gear or items, allowing naked fast travel directly from a Hideout to a Royal City or Island
Intended as an optimization of the "Reset Home" status effect introduced with Queen Patch 3, without requiring players to use /suicide
Non-tradable items now no longer factor into fast travel costs
sections.searchable_text: Fast Travel Home preset naked gear items Hideout Royal City Island Reset Home Queen Patch 3 suicide non-tradable items fast travel costs

sections.heading: Hideout Item Retrieval
items: Players can now retrieve items from Hideouts to which they no longer have access by clicking on the Hideout board. The process takes 30 days and costs silver.
sections.searchable_text: Hideout Item Retrieval access Hideout board 30 days silver

sections.heading: Resource Changes
sections.searchable_text: Resource Changes Tier 7 Tier 8 small nodes spawn charged respawn time Hide Critter carcasses despawn

subsections.heading: Resource Node Changes
subsections.searchable_text: Small Tier 7 T7 nodes 3 charges respawn 26100 78300 seconds tripled Tier 8 T8 2 charges respawn 68300 126600 doubled Hide Critter carcasses 600 86400 seconds despawn

changes.raw_text: Small Tier 7 nodes now spawn fully charged (3 charges). Respawn time tripled: 26100 seconds → 78300 seconds.
Small Tier 8 nodes now spawn fully charged (2 charges). Respawn time doubled: 68300 seconds → 126600 seconds.
Hide and Critter carcasses despawn after 600 seconds instead of 86400 seconds.

sections.heading: Randomized Dungeon Loot Changes
items: On average, the number of Black Market items a chest gives out should be the same for all tiers, but with an increased chance for higher-tier items in higher-tier chests
This change will reduce the drop chance for high tier items so that the overall value of the chest doesn't change
Loot lists were updated to reflect the changes done to artifacts and their decreased item value
This also affects loot from mobs
sections.searchable_text: Randomized Dungeon Loot Changes Black Market items chests tiers higher-tier drop chance artifacts item value mobs

sections.heading: Other Changes
items: Significantly reduced transmutation times, with the most significant reductions at higher tier and enchantment levels
Decreased base item value of Runes, Souls, and Relics by 75% to match the item power reduction
Bugfix: changed the Royal Sigil item value to match the Artifact Level 1 values as intended — changes repair costs while leaving item power unaffected
sections.searchable_text: Other Changes transmutation times higher tier enchantment Runes Souls Relics base item value 75% Royal Sigil Artifact Level 1 repair costs item power

sections.heading: Fixes
items: Fixed an issue where siege camp timers were accidentally reset to 14:00 UTC — they will now all be either 18:00 UTC or 03:00 UTC as intended
Farming: fixed an issue where a "you will lose progress" warning was displayed whether or not progress would be lost
Fixed an issue where the first letter of the player name was offset when using the large nameplate template
Removed a wrongly placed Siege Camp from Rivercopse Crossing — items in the chest when the camp is deleted will be retrievable from Lymhurst
Fixed an issue where some static open-world dungeons were classified as passages, which incorrectly allowed use of mounts inside them
Fixed an issue where the Crystal League UI would display a chest one level too high for an upcoming match entered from a territory
Fixed an issue where Hideout nutrition could not be filled via equipped food
Additional minor graphical, terrain, animation, UI, audio, and localization fixes
sections.searchable_text: Fixes siege camp timers 14:00 UTC 18:00 UTC 03:00 UTC Farming progress warning nameplate Rivercopse Crossing Siege Camp Lymhurst dungeons passages mounts Crystal League UI chest territory Hideout nutrition food graphical terrain animation UI audio localization
